markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Madrid be 22°C on May 6?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Adolfo Suárez Madrid-Barajas Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 6 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Adolfo Suárez Madrid-Barajas Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/es/madrid/LEMD.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

This trader consensus—44.5% implied probability for 21°C, 31.5% for 20°C, and 18.5% for 22°C—reflects May 5's cooler start (mid-morning readings ~15°C at Barajas Airport, forecast max 19°C) and recent mild trends (May 4 high ~20°C), keeping outcomes clustered near climatological early-May averages of 21–22°C. Watch for afternoon AEMET updates or evening model runs, as small shifts in cloud cover or winds could tip the peak by 1–2°C before resolution via official observations.

events.title: IEM Cologne Major 2026 Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the IEM Cologne 2026 tournament, currently scheduled for June 2nd - June 21st, 2026.

If this tournament is cancelled, postponed after July 1, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or a winner has not been declared within this timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

If multiple teams are declared winners, this market will resolve in favor of the team whose listed team name comes first alphabetically.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the tournament organizer, ESL (https://pro.eslgaming.com/). However, a consensus of credible reporting (e.g., Liquipedia at https://liquipedia.net/counterstrike/Intel_Extreme_Masters/2026/Cologne) may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Team Vitality commands 57.5% trader consensus as IEM Cologne Major 2026 favorite, fueled by their unmatched 2026 dominance—five straight S-tier titles, including a 3-0 BLAST Rivals Season 1 grand final sweep over Natus Vincere in March. Yet, a narrow escape against G2 with a stand-in and a 2-1 group stage defeat to Falcons at IEM Rio last month (ending an 18-match win streak) highlight vulnerabilities, boosting Falcons to 12.5% with three consecutive H2H victories since November 2025 amid roster flux rumors like Kyxan exit and Karrigan arrival. Natus Vincere (6.3%) and Team Spirit (5.5%) hold solid positioning via consistent playoffs runs and HLTV top rankings, while the field eyes RMR outcomes in the lead-up to June's Swiss stages and Lanxess Arena playoffs.

markets.question: Will Valtteri Bottas be the 2026 F1 Drivers' Champion?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

events.title: F1 Drivers' Champion
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by February 28, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Kimi Antonelli's commanding victory at the Miami Grand Prix on May 3 extended his drivers' championship lead to 100 points, 20 clear of Mercedes teammate George Russell, fueling trader consensus at 40.4% implied probability for the 2026 title. The 19-year-old Italian's three wins—from China and recent poles—highlight his rapid adaptation to the frontrunning Mercedes, outpacing Russell's Australian opener success amid the Briton's setup struggles and P4 Miami finish. Lando Norris (11.1%) and Charles Leclerc trail with 51 and 59 points, respectively, as McLaren and Ferrari lag in constructors', while Max Verstappen's lowly 26 points reflect Red Bull's woes, positioning the Mercedes duo as clear frontrunners early in the 24-race calendar.

markets.question: US x Cuba military clash in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a military encounter between the military forces of the United States and Cuba between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A "military encounter" is defined as any incident involving the use of force such as missile strikes, artillery fire, exchange of gunfire, or other forms of direct military engagement between US and Cuban military forces. Non-violent actions, such as warning shots, artillery fire into uninhabited areas, or missile launches that land in territorial waters or pass through airspace, will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution. Intentional ship ramming that results in significant damage to (e.g., a hole in the hull) or the sinking of a military ship by another will count toward a "Yes" resolution, however minor damage (scrapes, dents) will not.

Note: the United States Coast Guard is considered part of the United States military, and the Cuban Border Guard is considered part of the Cuban military.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent Trump administration actions, including a May 2 executive order expanding sanctions on Cuba's energy and defense sectors alongside the president's Palm Beach remarks hinting at a swift military takeover post-Iran operations and joking about deploying the USS Abraham Lincoln near Cuban shores, have prompted Cuban denunciations of "unprecedented" threats and Revolutionary Armed Forces readiness signals invoking the "War of the Entire People" doctrine. Pentagon contingency planning accelerated in April amid oil curbs since January, yet no deployments, airspace violations, or naval incidents have occurred, with U.S. officials emphasizing diplomacy and economic pressure. Traders' 56% consensus for no clash reflects historical restraint, absent direct provocation, and competing priorities like Iran peace talks, though rhetoric sustains uncertainty through year-end.

markets.question: Will Renan Santos finish in second place in the first round of the 2026 Brazilian presidential election?
markets.description:
A presidential election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate who receives the second-most valid votes in the first round of this election.

The named candidates will be primarily ranked by the number of valid votes received in the specified election. If two or more candidates are tied on valid votes, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the candidates' last names. This market will resolve to the candidate that occupies the second-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., https://dadosabertos.tse.jus.br/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent polls from late April, including AtlasIntel (April 22–27) showing Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva at 47% and Flávio Bolsonaro at 40% in stimulated first-round scenarios, alongside Nexus and Datafolha surveys, position Lula as the incumbent leader but Flávio as the clear runner-up, consolidating right-wing support amid fragmented opposition. Polymarket traders reflect this consensus with Flávio at 62.5% implied probability for second place on October 4, while pricing Lula's drop to second at 19.5% due to his age-related health concerns and tightening gaps. Lower odds for Camilo Santana (6.6%), Fernando Haddad (4.3%), and Renan Santos (4.9%) stem from their weaker polling in multi-candidate fields, with a potential runoff looming if no one exceeds 50% plus a 10-point margin.

markets.question: Will Bukayo Saka win the 2026 PFA Player of the Year?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the 2025-26 PFA Players' Player of the Year.

If two or more players are announced as winners of the 2026 PFA Players’ Player of the Year award, this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If no 2025-2026 PFA Players' Player of the Year winner is declared by August 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source will be official information from the Professional Footballers’ Association; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Bruno Fernandes commands 64% trader consensus as the 2025-26 PFA Players' Player of the Year frontrunner, propelled by his Premier League-leading 19 assists and 8 goals for third-placed Manchester United, where his 8.02 FotMob rating and creative dominance have created a massive statistical gap over rivals in recent weeks. Late-April surges vaulted him past prior favorite Declan Rice (24%), whose midfield control with 4 goals and 5 assists has fueled Arsenal's table-topping title charge amid tight standings. William Saliba (9.8%) bolsters Arsenal's backline with 11 clean sheets, while Erling Haaland's 25-goal haul (7.6%) sustains Manchester City's second-place bid despite lagging in broader contributions. With the season's final matches looming, Fernandes' all-season impact defines the wisdom of crowds.

markets.question: FaZe roster change Before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player joins or leaves FaZe Clan’s active Counter-Strike 2 roster (defined as the active lineup listed on the FaZe Clan Liquipedia page: https://liquipedia.net/counterstrike/FaZe_Clan)  before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

As of market creation the active roster consists of the following players: broky, frozen, jcobbb, and Twistzz. Name changes or aliases referring to the same player do not qualify as roster changes.

For the purpose of this market, "roster change" refers to any official signing, transfer, benching to inactive/reserve, release, retirement, or departure of a player from the active lineup. This includes adding new players (transfers, free agent signings, or loans), removing players (to inactive, free agency, or other teams), or any change resulting in a different active roster.

Coach changes and temporary stand-ins for a single match will not count.

If no qualifying roster change is confirmed at the time GTA VI is released, this market will resolve to “No” immediately.

For the purposes of this market, “release” refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the United States. Early access, beta versions, or leaks do not qualify. A release limited to specific platforms (e.g., console-only) will qualify.

The primary resolution sources will be official information from Liquipedia, Rockstar Games, or Take-Two Interactive; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: FaZe Clan's CS2 roster turmoil drives the 85% implied probability for a change before GTA VI's November 19 release, as traders price in ongoing instability from a dismal 2026 start. Legendary IGL karrigan departed for Team Falcons in late April after FaZe missed the latest Major via a shocking VRS collapse, prompting Neityu as stand-in with Twistzz trialing IGL duties. New coach Niclas “enkay J” Krumhorn replaced NEO amid whispers of frozen's exit and a potential rebuild targeting talents like woxic and mail09. Subpar form—few wins against top teams—fuels expectations of further roster moves in the crowded transfer window before the distant deadline.

markets.question: Fed rate hike in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is increased at any point between January 1, 2026 and the Fed's December 2026 meeting, currently scheduled for December 8-9, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may not resolve to "No" until the Fed has released its rate change decision following its December meeting.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Federal Reserve (https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders assign a 76.5% implied probability to no Federal Reserve rate hike in 2026, reflecting consensus that recent inflation pressures—driven by a war-related oil shock pushing March 2026 CPI to 3.3% year-over-year, up from 2.4%—will prove transitory amid steady federal funds rate targeting 3.50%-3.75%. The FOMC's April 29 decision to hold rates unchanged, despite minutes revealing some officials' openness to hikes, underscores a data-dependent stance prioritizing labor market resilience and core inflation trends over headline spikes. Upcoming April CPI release on May 12 and June FOMC meeting loom as key catalysts, with CME FedWatch futures showing minimal near-term tightening priced in through year-end.

markets.question: Will S&P 500 (SPY) hit (HIGH) $780 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for S&P 500 (SPY) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the S&P 500 (SPY) "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.SPY%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.SPY%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

events.title: What will S&P 500 (SPY) hit in May 2026?
events.description: What will S&P 500 (SPY) hit in May 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Strong Q1 2026 earnings growth of 27.1% for S&P 500 companies—the highest since Q4 2021—along with blended net profit margins of 13.4%, has driven the index to a record high of 7,272 on May 1, with SPY trading around 722 amid sustained gains into May 5 near 7,260. The Federal Reserve's April decision to maintain the federal funds target range at 3.5%-3.75% amid sticky inflation and resilient labor markets supports equity sentiment, despite notable policymaker dissent. Traders monitor remaining earnings reports, ADP employment data on May 6, nonfarm payrolls, and the June 16-17 FOMC meeting for signals on rate cuts that could influence month-end positioning.

markets.question: Will Anthony Hudson win the 2026 Michigan Governor Republican primary election?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for Governor of Michigan, scheduled to take place on August 4, 2026. Resolution will be based on the overall winner of the primary, including any potential second round or run-off.

If no 2026 Michigan Gubernatorial Republican Primary takes place, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement of the results from the Michigan Republican Party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

events.title: Michigan Governor Republican Primary Winner
events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors self-funded businessman Perry Johnson at 65% implied probability to win Michigan's Republican gubernatorial primary on August 4, 2026, ahead of U.S. Rep. John James at 32%, reflecting bets on Johnson's aggressive ad spending and appeal to the MAGA base despite recent polls. A Detroit Regional Chamber/Glengariff survey from late April showed James leading 37%-20% among likely primary voters, with former AG Mike Cox at 10%, but traders anticipate Johnson's heavy self-financing—echoing his near-frontrunner status in 2022 before a signature scandal—will boost turnout among core Republicans in this low-information primary. Evan Space garners minor support as a perennial challenger, while field fragmentation aids the top two; upcoming candidate filings and debates could shift dynamics.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins this election.

Temporary, interim, or placeholder governors appointed by any means before the specified election will not be considered.

If the result of this election isn't known by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., www.tse.jus.br/eleicoes/resultados-eleicoes).

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent Genial/Quaest polling from late April highlights the tight Ceará gubernatorial contest ahead of the October 4 first round and potential runoff, showing Ciro Gomes leading incumbent Elmano de Freitas 41%-32% in first-round matchups while trailing senator Camilo Santana 39%-44% in a second-round scenario. Trader consensus reflects this, with Ciro at 43% implied probability due to his consistent lead across surveys like Datafolha and Paraná Pesquisas, but Camilo's 36% pricing captures PT speculation on replacing Elmano to bolster runoff chances. Eduardo Girão's 28% underscores right-wing consolidation potential despite sub-10% polls. PT nominee dynamics, endorsements, and national polarization could tip the balance in this closely contested race.

markets.question: Will US crude oil reserves fall to 325M by June 5?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the U.S. Energy Information Administration publishes a weekly figure for U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve less than or equal to the specified value for any week ending on or before June 5, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve as soon as the listed value is reached, or once data has been released for the final week ending on or before June 5, 2026, and the listed value has not been reached.

If data has not been released for the final week ending on or before June 5 2026, by June 12, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on the data available at that time.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the U.S. Energy Information Administration, specifically the weekly data published for the U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve at https://www.eia.gov/dnav/pet/hist/LeafHandler.ashx?n=PET&s=WCSSTUS1&f=W.

Note: this market’s resolution source publishes weekly values of U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve in thousands of barrels. Thus, this will be the level of specificity used to resolve this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: U.S. Strategic Petroleum Reserve (SPR) crude oil stocks, tracked weekly by the EIA, have declined sharply to 398 million barrels as of the week ending early May 2026, down from 405 million the prior week, due to Department of Energy emergency exchanges of up to 172 million barrels to counter high oil prices amid U.S.-Iran tensions and Strait of Hormuz disruptions. This recent draw positions traders to favor outcomes at or below 400 million barrels by June 5, with further releases under ongoing DOE loan programs likely to pressure levels lower. Key factors include sustained U.S. production near record highs, surging exports, and refinery utilization above 90%, alongside upcoming EIA reports on May 6, 13, 20, 27, and June 3 that could confirm additional declines before resolution. OPEC+ ministerial meeting on June 7 follows market close.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Lucknow be 35°C on May 5?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Chaudhary Charan Singh Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 5 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Chaudhary Charan Singh Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/in/lucknow/VILK.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: India Meteorological Department observations and forecasts underpin the 100% market-implied probability for Lucknow's highest temperature on May 5 reaching 33°C or below at Chaudhary Charan Singh International Airport. Yesterday's maximum of 28°C—11.4°C below normal—stemmed from persistent cloud cover and thundery development linked to a western disturbance, with early morning readings today at 21°C amid 100% humidity and northeasterly winds. IMD projects a daytime high around 31°C under partly cloudy skies turning generally cloudy with rain/thundershower spells, curbing solar insolation and convective heating. While May climatology averages 38–41°C, current unsettled conditions suppress extremes; a challenge would require improbable afternoon clearing for rapid warming, as model consensus holds through sunset monitoring.

markets.question: Will Giravanz Kitakyūshū win on 2026-05-06?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for May 6, 2026
If Giravanz Kitakyūshū wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Giravanz Kitakyūshū vs. Roasso Kumamoto
events.description: This event is for the upcoming J2 100 Year Vision League game, scheduled for Wednesday, May 6, 2026 between Giravanz Kitakyūshū and Roasso Kumamoto.

eventMetadata.context_description: Giravanz Kitakyūshū enters as trader consensus slight favorite at home in the MEIJI YASUDA J2/J3 100 Year Vision League Matchweek 15 clash against higher-standing Roasso Kumamoto, driven by strong historical head-to-head dominance (8 wins to 3, 5-7 draws) and Mikuni World Stadium Kitakyushu advantage. Recent developments temper enthusiasm: Giravanz sit 9th after a 3-0 loss to Tegevajaro Miyazaki last weekend and a 2-2 penalty shootout exit versus Renofa Yamaguchi on April 24, marking mixed form (W L D L W). Roasso, 5th-6th with 13 points from 11 games, impressed with a 4-1 home win over Giravanz in February but stumbled in a 0-5 defeat and other inconsistencies (W L L W L). Tight odds reflect a closely contested table battle with draw potential amid both sides' erratic recent results and no major injury updates.

markets.question: Will Trump meet with Kim Jong Un in May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual meets with Donald Trump between May 1, and May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A meeting is defined as any encounter where both the listed individual and Trump are present and interact with each other in person.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump's confirmed bilateral summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping in Beijing on May 14-15, rescheduled from April amid escalating U.S.-Iran tensions over the Strait of Hormuz, forms the dominant driver of trader consensus in this multi-outcome market tracking in-person meetings with specified figures through May 31. Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi arrived in Beijing today for talks with Chinese counterparts on regional developments, potentially shaping the summit's Middle East agenda. Reports indicate Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva may meet Trump in Washington this Thursday. Early White House schedules list only domestic events like the May 5 small business summit, with speculation on Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu or others unconfirmed amid ongoing diplomacy.

markets.question: Will Gardner Minshew start Week 1 for the Chiefs in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed player who is the starting quarterback for the Kansas City Chiefs in their Week 1 regular season game of the 2026-2027 NFL season.

If the Chiefs do not name a single player as the starting quarterback, the market will resolve based on the player who takes the most snaps at quarterback during their first offensive drive of the game. If multiple players take the same number of snaps on the Chiefs’ first offensive drive, the market will resolve to the player who took the first snap at quarterback.

This market may not resolve before the start of the Chiefs’ Week 1 regular season game. 

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the NFL (NFL.com); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Patrick Mahomes leads at 61% implied probability as the Chiefs' Week 1 starting QB for 2026, fueled by recent ACL rehab updates showing him ahead of schedule—throwing passes and on track for OTAs after December 2025 surgery—bolstering trader confidence in his timely return despite typical nine-month timelines. Justin Fields sits at 25.5% following his March trade from the Jets as primary insurance, with Andy Reid's April endorsement calling him a "legitimate starting NFL QB" ready for early reps if needed. Chris Oladokun's 24.9% reflects his entrenched practice squad role and offseason buzz as a surprising contender over Fields, per mid-April reports, while veterans Joe Flacco (18.8%) and Gardner Minshew (8.1%) linger as free-agent fallback options amid roster depth including draft pick Garrett Nussmeier.

events.title: NBA Playoffs: Eastern Conference Champion

eventMetadata.context_description: New York Knicks lead Polymarket's Eastern Conference champion market at 43.5% implied probability after crushing the Philadelphia 76ers 137-98 in Game 1 of their conference semifinals on May 4, extending a historic postseason roll sparked by a first-round comeback against the Hawks. Jalen Brunson's 35-point outburst highlighted Knicks' offensive firepower and home-court edge as the No. 3 seed, boosting trader consensus on their path through a vulnerable 76ers squad (7.8%), where Joel Embiid played through a probable right hip contusion but faltered. No. 1 Detroit Pistons (25.7%) hold strong as top seed with home-court versus No. 4 Cleveland Cavaliers (22.5%) in tonight's Game 1, though backup Kevin Huerter remains questionable with a left abductor strain; Cavs advanced via grueling seven-game series win over Raptors, fueling competitive pricing amid balanced semis matchups.

markets.question: Spread: RC Strasbourg Alsace (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming Ligue 1 game, scheduled for May 10 at 3:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "RC Strasbourg Alsace" if RC Strasbourg Alsace win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Angers SCO".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: RC Strasbourg Alsace hold an advantageous position in the Ligue 1 table around 8th with 46 points from 30 matches, fueled by a dramatic 3-2 injury-time comeback victory at Lorient on April 26 that highlights their resilience amid a mixed run including losses to Rennes and Nice. Angers SCO, mired in 13th on 34 points from 31 games, struggle with poor recent form—drawing 1-1 versus Le Havre, losing 0-3 to PSG, and conceding heavily to Rennes and Lens—exacerbated by key injuries to Carlens Arcus (groin), Djibirin Harouna (ankle), and Yassin Belkhdim (arm). Strasbourg dominated earlier this season 5-0 at home, though Angers benefit from Stade Raymond Kopa home advantage and potential rest before matchday 33 on May 10. Trader consensus reflects Strasbourg's superior standing, momentum, and squad depth despite their own absences like Emanuel Emegha (muscle tear).

markets.question: Will Anže Logar be the next Prime Minister of Slovenia?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Slovenia on March 22, 2026.

This market will resolve to the next individual who is officially elected by the National Assembly (Državni zbor) and sworn in as Prime Minister of Slovenia following the next parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally sworn in. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is sworn in by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Slovenia; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following Robert Golob's Freedom Movement's narrow election win but subsequent failure to secure a coalition in April, President Nataša Pirc Musar declined to nominate a prime minister-designate after consultations revealed no clear National Assembly majority. Janez Janša's Slovenian Democratic Party (SDS), with 28 seats, has advanced center-right coalition talks with the NSi-SLS-FOKUS bloc (led by Jernej Vrtovec, 9 seats) and Democrats (led by Anže Logar, 6 seats), demonstrated by parliament's recent 49-41 vote to trim ministries from 19 to 14—a key SDS precondition. On May 5, Janša confirmed agreement on coalition guidelines, with details pending, driving trader consensus at 93% implied probability. Challenges could arise from Resni.ca's (Zoran Stevanović) reluctance, failure to muster 46 investiture votes, or resulting snap election.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Shanghai be 27°C on May 6?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Shanghai Pudong International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 6 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Shanghai Pudong International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/cn/shanghai/ZSPD.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket closely splits between 25–27°C highs for Shanghai on May 6, mirroring ensemble forecast spreads from China Meteorological Administration (CMA), GFS, and ECMWF models projecting peaks of 26–28°C under a high-pressure ridge and light southerly winds. Recent CMA updates show sunny-to-overcast conditions with 82°F (28°C) potential, boosted by post-frontal warming following May 5's clear skies and highs near 27°C, while timeanddate.com aligns at 27°C mostly sunny. Differentiating factors include sea breeze moderation from the East China Sea, possible afternoon cloud development, and urban heat island effects amplifying readings by 1–2°C above rural stations. Early May climatology averages 24–25°C, but this warm spell elevates odds; monitor CMA afternoon bulletins for shifts.

markets.question: Will Abelardo de la Espriella  win the 2026 Colombian presidential election?
markets.description:
Colombia's presidential elections are scheduled for May 31, 2026, and a second round (if required) on June 21, 2026, in case no candidate secures more than 50% of the valid votes in the first round.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate that wins this election. 

This market includes any potential second round. If the result of this election isn't known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices unidentified moderate Candidate M as the slim 49.5% favorite to win Colombia's 2026 presidential election, ahead of leftist Iván Cepeda Castro (35.5%), right-wing Abelardo de la Espriella (33.0%), and center-right Paloma Valencia (29.9%), in a fragmented field likely headed to a June 28 runoff after the May 31 first round. Recent polls like CNC (May 3) show Cepeda leading first-round voting at 37%, with de la Espriella at 20% and Valencia at 16%, but traders boost right-wing odds amid security concerns, economic pressures, and backlash to President Petro's uneven tenure. March congressional elections strengthened Petro's Historic Pact in Congress, yet polarization favors moderate appeal for Candidate M among wary voters.

markets.question: Will Harry Kane be the 2025/2026 top UCL goal scorer?

If a player's team is eliminated, and they are not in the goal lead, the market for that player may resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the UEFA Champions League. However, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

Khvicha Kvaratskhelia lags at 10 goals for PSG, reflecting his team's advancement needs and the steep catch-up required, while others like Julián Álvarez remain distant after early exits.

Greece's Akylas ("Ferto") and Denmark's Søren Torpegaard Lund ("Før vi går hjem") hold strong second and third positions, buoyed by dynamic visuals and OGAE poll momentum, while Australia's Delta Goodrem ("Eclipse") lurks as a televote threat. With national selections complete and 35 entries confirmed amid boycotts from Spain, Iceland, and others over Israel's participation, focus shifts to second rehearsals next week, the Turquoise Carpet on May 10, and Semi-Final 1 on May 12—key catalysts for late momentum swings.

markets.question: Will Racing Bulls get pole position at the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket on the constructor team that achieves pole position at the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix, scheduled for May 23, 2026.

If the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix is canceled or rescheduled to a date after May 30, 2026, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve in favor of the constructor team that is officially recognized by Formula 1 as having set the fastest time during the qualifying session for the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix. The market will be settled based on the FIA's official qualifying results, regardless of any subsequent penalties, disqualifications, or changes to the starting grid.

For example, if a constructor team sets the fastest qualifying time but later receives a grid penalty or is moved down the starting order, the market will still resolve to “Yes” for that constructor team.

The resolution source will be the official Formula 1 website and a consensus of credible sports news reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mercedes' single-lap dominance, securing all poles through Japan with Kimi Antonelli and George Russell, has propelled them to the constructors' lead at 135 points after Miami, yet trader consensus prices every team near even at 49.5% for Canadian Grand Prix constructor pole position amid the 2026 regs' parity push. Circuit Gilles Villeneuve's razor-thin margins, punishing kerbs, and Wall of Champions chicane amplify unpredictability, favoring clean laps over raw pace as track evolution and clean air prove decisive. Ferrari trails closely at 90 points with strong Q3 threats from Leclerc and Hamilton, while midfield risers like Haas and Alpine eye upgrades; newcomers Audi Revolut and Cadillac lag but could surprise in low-downforce setup.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the IL-09 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the IL-09 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors the Democratic Party at 92.5% in the IL-09 House race due to the district's strong D+19 Cook Partisan Voter Index and Evanston Mayor Daniel Biss's decisive win in the competitive March 17 Democratic primary, where he secured 29% amid a crowded field following Jan Schakowsky's retirement from the open seat. The Republican nominee, John Elleson, emerged from a low-turnout primary with just 12,000 votes, underscoring limited GOP strength in this safe Democratic stronghold rated Solid D by Cook Political Report. With no major developments in the past 30 days and no general election polls yet, scenarios like a Biss scandal, massive Republican national wave, or independent Delila Barrera surge could shift odds, though historical precedents for such safe seats suggest stability ahead of the November 3 general election.

Late-season stakes heighten upset potential for the visitors.

markets.question: Will Russia enter Shevchenko by May 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures any territory of Shevchenko, Donetsk Oblast, (48.384157880601364° N, 37.10809761679785° E) between market creation and the specified date (ET).

Territory will be considered captured if any part of the specified territory is shaded under a below specified layer on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. Otherwise, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either "Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine", “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. 

Once a qualifying condition is met, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

If Russia comes into control of the specified territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. However, an announcement of a negotiated settlement that gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.

Any continuous shading which reflects either "Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine", “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. 

Once a qualifying condition is met, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

If Russia comes into control of the specified territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. However, an announcement of a negotiated settlement that gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Russian forces continue intense probing assaults northwest of Pokrovsk in Donetsk Oblast, targeting Shevchenko as part of the broader Pokrovsk offensive to encircle the logistical hub. Ukrainian General Staff reported 65 Russian attacks on May 5, including near Shevchenko, Serhiivka, and Bilyakivka, with defenses holding amid counterstrikes using drones against Russian airborne elements like the 76th VDV Division. Russian Ministry of Defense claimed liberation of Shevchenko on May 2 alongside Sukhaya Balka and Tarasovka, but independent geolocations and ISW assessments through May 4 show no confirmed advances into the settlement, only continued pressure from northern Pokrovsk outskirts. Traders monitor OSINT maps for entry verification, with Ukrainian reinforcements and pending Western aid potentially stalling escalation.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

markets.question: Will Ousmane Dembele be the top goalscorer at the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the player who scores the most goals through all main tournament rounds of the 2026 FIFA World Cup competition.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official leader as determined by FIFA rules. If multiple leaders are announced then this market will resolve to the player who scored fewer goals from penalty kicks. If a tie still persists, this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is cancelled, postponed after August 2, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is no leader declared within that timeframe, the corresponding market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from FIFA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Kylian Mbappé holds a slim edge as trader consensus favorite for the 2026 FIFA World Cup Golden Boot at 20% implied probability, fueled by his blistering early-2026 form at Real Madrid with 9 goals in 7 matches and 24 LaLiga strikes this season, underscoring his pace and finishing for a France side eyeing deep knockout progression. Harry Kane trails closely at 15.5% after a prolific Bayern Munich campaign of 33 Bundesliga goals, leveraging penalty expertise and England's favorable path. The bunched top reflects intense competition from Lamine Yamal's breakout Spain exploits, Erling Haaland's record 16 UEFA qualifier goals despite Norway's limitations, Lionel Messi's Argentina pedigree amid fitness queries, and the expanded 48-team format amplifying upset potential and shared scoring loads across group stages and beyond.

markets.question: Spread: Club Independiente Petrolero (-1.5)
markets.description:

This market will resolve to "Club Independiente Petrolero" if Club Independiente Petrolero win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Caracas FC".

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the NV-01 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NV-01 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

Dina Titus's commanding fundraising lead—$1.5 million raised and over $1 million cash-on-hand—over Democratic primary challengers Joy Hoover, Gabriel Cornejo, and Luis Paniagua bolsters trader consensus for a Democratic hold at 84% in Nevada's 1st Congressional District, a D+2 partisan lean area. Recent April 29 reporting highlights intra-party criticism of Titus's Laken Riley Act vote amid calls for generational change, but her historical general election margins (52% in 2024) and weak challenger resources minimize primary risks ahead of June 9. A fragmented Republican primary field, including state Sen. Carrie Buck and others, limits GOP viability per Likely Democratic ratings from Cook and Sabato's Crystal Ball.

markets.question: Will the Eurozone's 2026 Annual Inflation be between 1.6% and 1.8%?
markets.description:
This is a market about the variation of consumer prices in the Eurozone over the 12-month period ending December 2026 as reported by Eurostat.

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in the Harmonised Index of Consumer Prices (HICP) over the 12-month period ending December 2026, according to the monthly Eurostat report.

The resolution source for this market will be the Eurostat Harmonised Index of Consumer Prices monthly report released for December 2026, currently scheduled to be released on January 19, 2027. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

You can find this report on the Eurostat website (https://ec.europa.eu/eurostat/en/) by selecting the "Database" option from the "Data" dropdown, opening the "Data navigation tree" folder, then the "Economy and finance" folder, then the "Prices (prc)" folder, then the "Harmonised index of consumer prices (HICP) (prc_hicp)" folder, and opening the file named "HICP - monthly data (annual rate of change) (prc_hicp_manr)". The relevant figure can be found in the column for the relevant month in the row marked "Euro area - 20 countries (from 2023)".

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly Eurostat HICP news release which reports inflation change over 12-month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 1.9%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market. For the full release schedule, see: https://ec.europa.eu/eurostat/web/main/news/release-calendar or https://www.ecb.europa.eu/press/calendars/statscal/ges/html/sthicp.en.html

eventMetadata.context_description: Eurozone HICP inflation surged to 3.0% year-over-year in April 2026—its highest since September 2023—driven by spiking energy costs from Middle East tensions, lifting trader consensus toward higher annual 2026 averages as reflected in Polymarket's 41.4% implied probability for 3.1%+ and 27.8% for 2.8-3.0%. March's 2.6% print had already exceeded ECB staff projections of 2.6% for full-year 2026 HICP, with the latest Survey of Professional Forecasters revising expectations to 2.7% amid wage persistence and core measures at 2.4%. ECB held deposit facility rate at 2.0% on April 30 but signaled June hike risks via hawks like Nagel and Kazimir, tempering rate-cut bets; full April data due May 20 and May HICP could further shape pricing before year-end resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: One Nation candidate David Farley's commanding 82% implied probability in the Farrer by-election stems from recent polls showing his primary vote strength around 30-53% and decisive preference flows from Liberal and Nationals voters, who have cross-endorsed him in the traditionally Coalition stronghold vacated by Sussan Ley. Independent Michelle Milthorpe trails at 18.5% despite solid 2025 performance and Climate 200 backing, hampered by open preferencing and anti-One Nation campaigns like GetUp!'s $600,000 spend. Early voting since April 28 has surged in the final week, with leaked Liberal polling naming Farley the "red hot favourite" amid water policy and regional healthcare debates, though One Nation infighting and candidate gaffes pose late risks before Saturday's poll. Minor candidates like Liberal's Raissa Butkowski languish due to vote fragmentation.

Alphabet trails closely at 30.6% trader consensus after surging ~$420 billion in market cap over the past week on blockbuster Q1 earnings—22% revenue growth to $110 billion and 63% expansion in Google Cloud—narrowing the gap to Nvidia to under $200 billion and fueling bets on its AI infrastructure momentum. Lower probabilities for Apple, Tesla, Amazon, Microsoft, and Saudi Aramco reflect their distant standings below $3 trillion, with no comparable recent catalysts; Nvidia's upcoming earnings on May 20 could solidify or shift positioning.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the open Democratic primary for New York's 12th Congressional District to succeed retiring Rep. Jerry Nadler, trader consensus reflects a dead heat between state Assemblymembers Micah Lasher at 36.5% and Alex Bores at 36%, with Jack Schlossberg trailing at 21.5% amid a crowded field. Gov. Kathy Hochul's April endorsement of her former policy advisor Lasher, coupled with Michael Bloomberg's March backing via a super PAC, has nudged him ahead of Bores, whose internal poll showed Schlossberg leading earlier. Recent analyses highlight voters aged 50+ comprising up to 75% of turnout, favoring experienced legislators over Schlossberg's viral appeal. Ongoing attack ads, forums on Trump opposition and foreign policy, plus impending public polls and fundraising disclosures ahead of the June 23 primary, could consolidate support and create separation in this closely contested race.

markets.question: Will Airbnb (ABNB) beat quarterly earnings?
markets.description:
As of market creation, Airbnb is estimated to release earnings on May 7, 2026. The Street consensus estimate for Airbnb's GAAP EPS for the relevant quarter is $0.30 as of market creation. This market will resolve to "Yes" if Airbnb reports GAAP EPS greater than $0.30 for the relevant quarter in its next quarterly earnings release. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No." The resolution source will be the GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents. 

If Airbnb releases earnings without GAAP EPS, then the market will resolve according to the GAAP EPS figure reported by SeekingAlpha. If no such figure is published within 96h of market close (4:00:00pm ET) on the day earnings are announced, the market will resolve to “No”.

If the company does not release earnings within 45 calendar days of the estimated earnings date, this market will resolve to “No.” 

Note: Subsequent restatements, corrections, or revisions made to the initially announced GAAP EPS figure will not qualify for resolution, except in the case of obvious and immediate mistakes (e.g., fat finger errors, as with Lyft's (LYFT) earnings release in February 2024).
Note: The strike prices used in these markets are derived from SeekingAlpha estimates, and reflect the consensus of sell-side analyst estimates for GAAP EPS. 
Note: All figures will be rounded to the nearest cent using standard rounding.
Note: For the purposes of this market, IFRS EPS will be treated as GAAP EPS.
Note: For the purposes of this market, GAAP EPS refers to diluted GAAP EPS, unless this is not published, in which case it refers to basic GAAP EPS.
Note: All figures are expressed in USD, unless otherwise indicated.
Note: For primarily internationally listed companies, this market refers specifically to the shares traded in the United States on U.S. stock exchanges such as the NYSE or Nasdaq. In cases where the company trades in the U.S. through an American Depositary Receipt (ADR) or American Depositary Share (ADS), this market will refer to the ADR/ADS.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 64% implied probability that Airbnb (ABNB) will not beat Q1 2026 consensus estimates of $0.30 EPS and $2.62 billion in revenue, due primarily to Zacks' negative Earnings Surprise Prediction (ESP) score signaling limited upside potential. This reflects softening occupancy trends reported by host analytics firm AirROI amid moderating travel demand, despite average daily rate (ADR) gains of 11-37%, which align with Airbnb's tight $2.59-2.63 billion revenue guidance issued in February. Q4 2025 results showed revenue growth of 12% but an EPS miss from tax charges, tempering optimism. Key catalyst: earnings release on May 7, with focus on nights booked and gross booking value growth.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mike Minogue's commanding 80% implied probability in the Massachusetts Republican gubernatorial primary stems from his landslide endorsement at the April 26 state GOP convention, where he captured 70% of delegates—well above the 15% threshold for ballot qualification—while surging ahead in fundraising with nearly $10 million cash on hand to fuel statewide ads. Brian Shortsleeve narrowly cleared the 15% mark to secure primary ballot access, positioning him as the main challenger at 11.7%, amid calls for debates ahead of the September 1 primary. Mike Kennealy's slim 1.1% reflects his failure to qualify at the convention, limiting his path despite earlier polling leads, as trader consensus prioritizes party backing and resources in this low-turnout primary.

markets.question: LoL: Disguised vs Dignitas (BO3) - LCS Regular Season
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Disguised and Dignitas in the LCS Regular Season, initially scheduled for May 9 at 7:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Disguised" if Disguised win the match against Dignitas.

This market will resolve to "Dignitas" if Dignitas win the match against Disguised.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Disguised enters this LCS regular season BO3 against Dignitas as the slight favorite among traders, reflecting their recent 2-0 upset win over DIG in Esports World Cup 2026 NA qualifiers that eliminated the opponent. Both squads languish at the bottom of Spring Split standings—Disguised with a 1-4 series record after a 0-3 loss to Team Liquid on May 2, and winless Dignitas reeling from sweeps by Shopify Rebellion and Cloud9 last week. Key factors include stagnant rosters, adaptation to the latest patch meta favoring aggressive early game, and desperation for series points to climb toward playoffs, with head-to-head history split but DSG's qualifier momentum tilting sentiment.

markets.question: Will Yoo Dong-soo win the 2026 Incheon mayoral election?

This market will resolve according to the candidate that wins this election.

If the result of this election isn't known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the South Korean government, specifically the National Election Commission (https://www.nec.go.kr/).

markets.question: Will Aħwa Maltin win the most seats in the House of Representatives in the 2026 Maltese general election?
markets.description:
General elections are scheduled to be held in Malta on May 30, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the House of Representatives (parliament of Malta) as a result of the 2026 Maltese general election.

If the results of this election are not known definitively by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

Parties will primarily be ranked by the number of seats won. In the event of a tie, the party with the greater number of valid votes will rank higher. If a tie persists, the party whose name appears first in alphabetical order will rank higher. This market will resolve to the party that occupies the highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the House of Representatives in the 2026 Maltese general election, not any coalition it may be a part of.

This market will resolve based on the results of the 2026 Maltese general election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve solely on the official results reported by the Government of Malta, such as those published by the Electoral Commission of Malta (https://electoral.gov.mt/).

markets.question: LoL: mCon esports vs The Bandits - Game 2 Winner
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between mCon esports and The Bandits in the Road Of Legends Regular Season, initially scheduled for May 5 at 2:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "mCon esports" if mCon esports win Game 2 against The Bandits.

This market will resolve to "The Bandits" if The Bandits win Game 2 against mCon esports.

If the match begins but is not completed, and Game 2 is concluded with a winner determined, this market will resolve based on the completed Game 2. 

If Game 2 is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50. 

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without play beginning, this market will resolve 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: LoL: mCon esports vs The Bandits (BO3) - Road Of Legends Regular Season
events.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between mCon esports and The Bandits in the Road Of Legends Regular Season, initially scheduled for May 5 at 2:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "mCon esports" if mCon esports win the match against The Bandits.

This market will resolve to "The Bandits" if The Bandits win the match against mCon esports.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Bandits enter their Road of Legends 2026 Spring regular season BO3 matchup against mCon esports unbeaten in their last four series, holding second place with a 4-1 record and +6 game differential after a sole 0-2 loss to undefeated leaders Frites Esports Club last week. mCon esports languish in sixth at 1-4, winless in their past four outings—including a 1-2 defeat to Myth Esports five days ago—desperately needing a win to bolster top-six playoff hopes in the single round-robin format. Head-to-head favors The Bandits, who downed mCon in Winter Split seeding, with both rosters stable amid a consistent meta; stylistic edges in macro play and bot lane duels could prove decisive in this pivotal group stage clash.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 600-619 tweets in May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X during the month of May 2026.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

markets.question: Will Matteo Berrettini win the 2026 Men's French Open?
markets.description:
The 2026 French Open is scheduled for May 18 - June 7, 2026.

This market will resolve to the player that wins the 2026 French Open Men’s Singles Tournament.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed player to win the 2026 French Open Men’s Singles Tournament per the rules of the tournament, the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2026 French Open Men’s Singles Tournament is cancelled, postponed after July 31, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the French Open (https://www.rolandgarros.com/en-us/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Carlos Alcaraz's withdrawal from the 2026 French Open due to a right wrist injury—confirmed April 24 after medical tests—has vaulted Jannik Sinner to 70.5% trader consensus as the clear Roland Garros favorite, opening the draw for the world No. 1 seeking his first clay Grand Slam. Sinner's dominance stems from his Madrid Masters 1000 title last week, crushing Alexander Zverev 6-1, 6-2 in the final while extending a 28-match Masters win streak and dropping just two sets over two months on clay. Zverev (7.1%) and Novak Djokovic (6.6%) trail as viable threats via proven clay records and experience, though Djokovic's lingering shoulder injury clouds his prep; rising Frenchman Arthur Fils (3.6%) gains from home support and recent Barcelona success amid a depleted field.

markets.question: Will SpaceX's market cap be between $1.8T and $2.0T at market close on IPO day?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on SpaceX's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no SpaceX IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO before 2028".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on SpaceX’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

events.description:
This market will resolve based on SpaceX's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no SpaceX IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO before 2028".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on SpaceX’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors a SpaceX IPO closing market cap exceeding $2.0 trillion at 55.5% implied probability, propelled by the company's confidential S-1 filing on April 1, 2026, and Bloomberg reports shortly after elevating the target valuation above $2 trillion from an initial $1.75 trillion benchmark. This surge reflects Starlink's satellite constellation scaling to dominate global broadband with projected 80% revenue growth to $18.7 billion in 2026, alongside Starship's Version 3 prototypes advancing reusable rocket milestones ahead of Flight 12 targeted for mid-May. Recent tender offers at $800 billion underscore the valuation leap, fueled by 50 orbital launches already this year; lower buckets trail as "No IPO before 2028" odds sit at just 3%, with public S-1 and June roadshow as key near-term catalysts amid potential market volatility.

markets.question: Arsenal FC leading at halftime?
markets.description: In the upcoming game, scheduled for May 10, 2026 If Arsenal FC wins within the first 45 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time, this market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No". This market refers only to the outcome within the first 45 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 24 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: West Ham United FC vs. Arsenal FC - Halftime Result
events.description: In the upcoming Premier League game between West Ham United FC and Arsenal FC, scheduled for May 10, 2026 at 11:30 AM ET: This event contains halftime result markets for home, draw, and away outcomes within the first 45 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices West Ham United FC, Draw, and Arsenal FC at 49.5¢ each for the halftime result, underscoring a fiercely competitive London derby where first-half caution prevails amid late-season stakes. West Ham, battling mid-table security with a gritty 2-1 home win over Everton on April 25 and a resilient 0-0 draw at Crystal Palace days earlier, leverage London Stadium's fortress-like atmosphere and solid home form against goal-shy opening periods. Title frontrunners Arsenal, who reclaimed Premier League summit via a narrow 1-0 victory versus Newcastle on the same weekend, face injury headwinds including Bukayo Saka's ankle issue, Martin Ødegaard's knee problem, and doubts over Declan Rice and Jurrien Timber, tempering their attacking fluency early. Head-to-head history shows tight first halves, amplifying the even-market dynamics.

markets.question: Will "Congress" be said during the first episode of the Joe Rogan Experience this week?
markets.description:
The Joe Rogan Experience podcast releases episodes on https://www.youtube.com/@joerogan.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed term is mentioned by anyone during the first released episode of the Joe Rogan Experience Podcast between May 4, 2026 and May 10, 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

If clips of old episodes or prerecorded clips are aired where people are speaking, those clips will count toward this market's resolution.

AI-generated audio or video will count toward this market's resolution.

Any usage of the term, regardless of context, will count toward the resolution of this market.

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

If no such episode of the Joe Rogan Experience Podcast is aired by May 10, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

JRE MMA Show episodes will not count for this market, only Joe Rogan Experience episodes.

The resolution source will be the released episode from (https://www.youtube.com/@joerogan).

markets.question: Will Ted Cruz post 40-59 posts from May 5 to May 12, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Ted Cruz (@tedcruz), posts on X between May 5, 12:00 PM ET and May 12, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.
